concerns.text: FR-001 mandates the Baseline agent be 'trained on standard linear trajectories ONLY'. Task T020 only defines 'Implement code/agents/baseline_agent.py (generic instruction following)' without a task to load, process, or train on the specific linear trajectory dataset. The experimental control condition is undefined.

FR-008 requires validating synthetic error patterns against a 'documented real-world error taxonomy' and citing the source. Tasks T009 and T013 only verify the URL/citation existence. There is no task to load, parse, or apply the taxonomy rules during benchmark generation to ensure the generated errors match the taxonomy.

SC-003 requires the report to include 'the count of discordant pairs' alongside the McNemar's test result. Task T028 only specifies implementing the test calculation. There is no explicit task to extract, store, or output the discordant pair count for the final report generation.

FR-001 requires the benchmark to use a 'hold-out set of error patterns disjoint from any used in the Hybrid agent's training data'. Task T014 mentions using 'disjoint error taxonomies' but lacks a specific task to implement the data separation logic (ensuring specific error instances used for Hybrid training are excluded from the benchmark generation).

SC-005 requires a sensitivity analysis where error-injection thresholds vary by ±10% to prove stability (<5% variance). Task T030 only mentions implementing the 'logic' for this analysis. There is no task to actually execute the perturbation runs and record the resulting success rate variance required to verify the criterion.

FR-002 requires executing agents using 'CPU-only inference with GGUF quantization (minimum 4-bit)'. Task T023 mentions '4-bit quantization (GGUF/CPU)' but does not explicitly include a task to verify or enforce the 7GB RAM limit during the loading/execution phase, which is a critical constraint for feasibility.
